video_title: Crypto’s Flow-Driven Market Has Arrived | Shaundadevens & Kunal Doshi
gist: 0xResearch argues crypto has entered a flow-driven, dispersion regime where TradFi/DAT/ETF flows — not fundamentals or cheap multiples — dictate which tokens (HYPE, Zcash, Morpho) outperform while BTC and majors bleed; the panel favors leaning into proven-strength names over contrarian value bets, and is bearish Ethena as basis-trade yields collapse and it devolves into a low-margin stablecoin wrapper.

themes.theme: BTC structural buyer base flipping from inflows to outflows

insights.claim: Bitcoin's two main 2024 price drivers — ETF inflows and MicroStrategy/Saylor buying — have reversed in 2026 to ETFs net selling while Saylor remains the only structural buyer.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: If the marginal BTC buyer is now a single concentrated actor, BTC's downside support is structurally thinner than in 2024 — watch ETF flow prints as the key tell.
insights.quote: if you look in the last like 28 days, the ETFs have sold more than Saylor bought, even though he's had like record strike selling and buying BTC

insights.claim: Over the trailing 28 days Bitcoin ETFs sold more BTC than Saylor bought, despite Saylor running record at-the-money/strike issuance to fund purchases.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: Net ETF supply is now negative even against record corporate buying — a quantitative bear signal for BTC flow balance.

insights.claim: BTC is down roughly 17% year-to-date and posted its worst day of ETF outflows of the year while Nasdaq, S&P 500 and AI equities rip, marking a decoupling of BTC from risk-on equities.
insights.speaker: host
insights.so_what: BTC underperforming on both up and down equity days suggests it has lost its risk-asset beta and become a flow-starved laggard.

insights.claim: Retail capital is being absorbed by semiconductor and AI equity plays, draining the marginal capital pool that would otherwise rotate into crypto markets.
insights.speaker: Kunal Doshi
insights.so_what: Crypto's bid is competing directly with the AI equity trade for retail dollars — a sustained AI rally is a headwind for broad crypto.

insights.claim: The S&P 500 is at overbought levels with put-to-call ratios heavily skewed toward calls, creating downside risk that would drag BTC lower if major indexes pull back.
insights.speaker: Kunal Doshi
insights.so_what: An equity-led risk-off event is the main near-term tail risk for BTC even though ETF outflows may already be front-running it.

themes.theme: Flow-driven dispersion regime: lean into strength, not cheap multiples

insights.claim: The 'don't hide in BTC' thesis (Austin Barrack) has been directionally correct as names like HYPE, Zcash, cards and Morpho decouple from majors on fundamentals and idiosyncratic catalysts.
insights.speaker: host
insights.so_what: In a dispersion tape, asset selection beats beta — holding BTC as a 'safe' crypto proxy has been the losing trade.
insights.quote: this is not the market to be hiding in BTC

insights.claim: BTC's historical edge as the only structural funnel for TradFi flows has eroded as ETH, SOL and now HYPE get their own ETFs and DATs, removing access scarcity as a reason to own BTC.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: With access democratized, allocation now hinges on fundamental growth/narrative rather than which asset TradFi can technically buy — reduces BTC's relative bid.
insights.quote: the argument that previously existed, which was BTC was the only one positioned to capture these tradfi flows, doesn't exist anymore

insights.claim: Shaundadevens advocates a momentum strategy: lean into proven-strength names that have already captured flows because institutions will bid those same winners with size, rather than buying optically cheap tokens whose multiples only look good because price bled for years.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: A reusable allocation rule: distinguish 'cheap because revenue grew' from 'cheap because price collapsed' and only buy the former; default to winners.
insights.quote: it's probably way better to lean into strength, lean into assets that have already proven like inside this crypto niche, they've gotten flows

insights.claim: Crypto valuations are decoupling from fundamentals just as TradFi equities trade at 100x+ price-to-sales, implying the market now pays premiums purely for credible 10-100x future revenue growth rather than current cheapness.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: Mean-reversion value screens are broken in this regime; underwrite forward revenue trajectory and flow access instead of trailing multiples.
insights.quote: It it just proves again that the flows are the only thing that matters

insights.claim: A value approach can still work in select crypto names (Meta DAO, Pendle, Aave) but only with a 1-3 year horizon, and better entry points are likely available than current prices.
insights.speaker: Kunal Doshi
insights.so_what: Long-horizon value buyers should wait for better entries; short-term capital is better deployed in momentum/flow names.

insights.claim: Hyperliquid's spot ETFs (21Shares, Bitwise) had the strongest debut of any crypto spot ETF, accumulating ~1% of circulating supply in the first 10 trading days versus ~0.6% for BTC's launch.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: HYPE is absorbing institutional demand faster than BTC did at launch — a quantifiable structural bid underpinning the token.
insights.quote: the ETFs accumulated 1% of the circulating supply compared to BTC that did like uh 0.6%

insights.claim: Hyperliquid Strategies (the HYPE DAT) bought $82M of HYPE in a single week — roughly 40 days of assistance-fund buying (~$2M/day) — far exceeding even the successful ETF inflows.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: DAT demand is the dominant marginal buyer for HYPE, ~40x the protocol's own assistance-fund buyback rate.
insights.quote: They bought like 40 days of assistance fund flows in a week

insights.claim: Hyperliquid Strategies trades at ~1.2x NAV and, in the same week it bought $82M of HYPE, grew its cash by $43.4M to $126M, signaling demand strong enough to fund buying without depleting cash.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: A sustained premium-to-NAV plus rising cash is a self-reinforcing accumulation flywheel and a likely TradFi access vehicle for HYPE.
insights.quote: they actually increased in the same week their cash by 43.4 million, and they now hold 126 million

insights.claim: FDV is the wrong valuation metric for HYPE because the ~38% earmarked for future emissions is unlikely to be released, or would be released positive-EV to holders; outstanding-supply market cap is ~$32B.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: Bears citing ~$50B FDV vs CME's ~$100B cap overstate HYPE's richness; circulating-supply structural demand/supply imbalance is the right lens.
insights.quote: FDV is obviously not the right metric to use to value hyperliquid supply

insights.claim: HYPE's DAT-driven rally differs from ETH's 2025 DAT pump because the DATs are neither the only buyer nor the only narrative — HYPE was already growing in revenue, had an assistance fund, and was trending up before DATs arrived.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: Don't expect an obvious 'DAT runs out of money' top in HYPE like ETH printed; more likely a range-then-range structure with less violent reversal.
insights.quote: I definitely don't think it's going to be such an obvious telegraph top as we saw on ETH

insights.claim: HYPE is struggling to break $60 because BTC weakness is capping alt upside; in a more risk-on tape HYPE could move materially higher.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: HYPE's near-term ceiling is a function of BTC beta; a BTC bounce is the unlock for a HYPE breakout above all-time highs.

insights.claim: TradFi's bull case for Hyperliquid is its simplicity: a blockchain powering 24/7 trading of any priced instrument (equities, pre-IPOs) that is extremely profitable and enables markets that don't exist in TradFi.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: The pitch resonates with institutions because it maps to a familiar, scalable exchange-revenue model — supports durable allocator demand.
insights.quote: It's a blockchain that's powering 24/7 trading of any equity asset, any instrument with a price feed, and it's just extremely profitable

themes.theme: Lending sector: Morpho premium, fixed-rate shift, looping risk repricing

insights.claim: Morpho's Midnight white paper introduces non-custodial fixed-rate, fixed-term credit markets using isolated permissionless markets with zero-coupon-like credit/debt units, pushing DeFi toward the structure needed to attract sophisticated lenders.
insights.speaker: host
insights.so_what: Fixed-term DeFi credit is the maturation path; Midnight positions Morpho alongside Maple and Sky in the institutional credit design space.

insights.claim: Morpho trades at over 100x price-to-sales (assuming a 10% take rate it does not currently charge) versus ~17-18x for Aave/Kamino/Euler and ~10x for Sky/Maple/Spark, an unjustified ~10x premium.
insights.speaker: host
insights.so_what: Morpho is the lending sector's most valuation-stretched name; a fee-switch activation would force a painful re-rating once revenue is visible.
insights.quote: I did some napkin numbers recently on Morpho and it was trading at over 100 X price to sales

insights.claim: Morpho's premium is partly justified by its Coinbase integration funneling retail onboarding, but a 10x premium over peers makes no sense and token unlocks ahead will add supply pressure.
insights.speaker: Kunal Doshi
insights.so_what: The narrative can carry Morpho higher until the fee switch flips; watch unlock schedule and any fee activation as the catalyst that ends the premium.

insights.claim: The Coinbase + Morpho Midnight integration's clearest near-term use case is retail BTC-collateral loans or fixed-rate stablecoin deposits (e.g. deposit BTC, borrow USDC at ~5% fixed for 6 months), not sophisticated looping.
insights.speaker: host
insights.so_what: Retail fixed-rate products via Coinbase are the realistic flow driver; no launch date for Midnight yet is the key thing to watch.

insights.claim: After the Aave drama where USDC suppliers effectively subsidized sUSD/Syrup looping, the market will demand higher rates for looping, shrinking the set of profitable looping assets and raising the cost to loop across DeFi.
insights.speaker: Kunal Doshi
insights.so_what: Looping-driven yield strategies (a core Ethena/sUSD demand source) get less profitable — a second-order drag on synthetic-dollar yields.

insights.claim: Euler stopped charging protocol fees (announced ~last week) to stay competitive with fee-free Morpho, illustrating that lending protocols can scale usage but struggle to capture economics without hurting competitiveness during growth mode.
insights.speaker: host
insights.so_what: Fee monetization is the structural weak point of DeFi lending; turning on fees risks deposit churn, capping realizable revenue and supporting the bear case on stretched multiples.

insights.claim: Aave saw large outflows post-drama that still haven't recovered, with capital migrating to Spark, Maple and Morpho or sitting idle in protocol treasuries waiting for more certain collateral and rate terms.
insights.speaker: Kunal Doshi
insights.so_what: Sidelined treasury capital is latent demand for fixed-rate/fixed-term products — first protocol to capture it (likely Morpho via Coinbase) wins material flows.

themes.theme: Ethena bear case: basis trade dead, devolving into stablecoin wrapper

insights.claim: Ethena's stablecoin supply has collapsed from a ~$17B peak (September last year) to just above $5B as basis-trade yields compressed.
insights.speaker: Kunal Doshi
insights.so_what: A ~70% supply contraction signals the core product's value proposition is broken — directly bearish for ENA fundamentals.
insights.quote: at the peak in September last day, it was 17B, and now it's just slightly above 5 billion

insights.claim: Ethena's yields have fallen from nearly 10% at the 2025 peak to ~3-4% as the cyclical market kills sustained basis-trade profitability and the strategy's replicability compresses returns as more capital enters.
insights.speaker: Kunal Doshi
insights.so_what: At 3-4%, sUSDe yields are barely above Treasuries while carrying more risk — weak incentive to hold absent token incentives.

insights.claim: Ethena increasingly allocates away from the basis trade into liquid cash, USDtb (Treasury yields) and incentive-driven lending deposits (MegaETH, Plasma, Jupiter, Kamino, USDG campaign), making it function like an on-chain asset manager rather than a synthetic-dollar issuer.
insights.speaker: Kunal Doshi
insights.so_what: Its current 3-4% yield leans on transient lending incentives; as those decay, yields likely fall further — reframe ENA as a low-margin allocator, not a yield engine.

insights.claim: With the basis trade no longer lucrative, Ethena has 'no choice' but to become a stablecoin wrapper, which the panel views as bearish given little value-add over safer yield-bearing stables.
insights.speaker: Shaundadevens
insights.so_what: Loss of the differentiated funding-yield product undercuts Ethena's investment case; a clear sector short/avoid signal.
insights.quote: their old strategy is not lucrative anymore and they've essentially become a stablecoin wrapper and I don't really see much value in that

insights.claim: RWA and equity basis trades cannot scale for a $5B protocol like Ethena: gold and even SOL basis trades lack the order-book depth (checked on Binance and Hyperliquid) to reverse during outflow events like the ~$1B redemption on 10/10.
insights.speaker: Kunal Doshi
insights.so_what: Diversification can't rescue Ethena's yields at scale; SOL never exceeded ~0.5% of reserve backing for the same liquidity reason.
insights.quote: it's just not possible to do it in size

insights.claim: Even Aave's proposed yield diversification (gold RWA basis trade via Centrifuge CLOs, institutional lending, USDC/Maple lending) backtests to only ~4-5% — more stable but not meaningfully higher than current basis-trade yields.
insights.speaker: Kunal Doshi
insights.so_what: Gold/CLO trades offer the best diversification from the BTC/ETH basis trade but won't restore double-digit DeFi yields — the high-yield-stablecoin era is structurally over.

themes.theme: Yield-bearing stablecoin landscape: Sky and Maple outpace Ethena

insights.claim: Maple's Syrup USDC and Sky's USDS have outperformed Ethena's sUSDe with simpler, safer strategies — Syrup lends to institutions against over-collateralized (mostly BTC) loans at roughly a 1% premium to lending markets.
insights.speaker: host
insights.so_what: Safer, transparent yield products are winning share from basis-trade dollars — favor Maple/Sky over Ethena in the synthetic-dollar trade.

insights.claim: Sky abstracts rate volatility via a governance-set Sky Savings Rate (~3.65%, above sUSDe), pushing the burden of generating excess spread onto capital allocators like Spark, Obex and Grove — a bank-deposit-like model.
insights.speaker: host
insights.so_what: Sky's fixed-rate UX plus allocator-borne spread risk is a more durable retail product; allocators (Spark) are where the real yield-generation alpha and risk sit.
